profiling_summary: Hotspots: SAXPY kernel is memory bandwidth bound, low arithmetic intensity, performance appears limited by cache/memory behavior.

ranked_candidates.target: SAXPY loop vectorization and memory access efficiency
ranked_candidates.rationale: Kernel is memory-bandwidth bound; ensure consistent autovectorization and generate optimal load/store streams by communicating alignment and aliasing assumptions to the compiler, and optionally enabling FMA/unrolling to reduce loop overhead.
ranked_candidates.action_sketch: Add restrict-qualified pointers in the hot loop scope and assert 64B alignment via compiler builtins; optionally add ivdep/simd pragmas to force vectorization. Build with -O3 -march=native (or tuned -mcpu) and compare with/without -ffast-math/-fno-math-errno depending on tolerance policy.
preconditions: Compiler supports alignment/assume intrinsics or pragmas (e.g., GCC/Clang)
Pointers x,y,z do not alias in practice and remain 64B-aligned as allocated
correctness_checks: Compare checksum and elementwise max-abs/max-rel error of z vs baseline within tolerance (e.g., 1e-12 rel) when enabling any fast-math-related flags
Verify no NaNs/Infs introduced for expected input ranges
performance_metrics: vectorization report confirms SIMD for the loop
rollback_criteria: Vectorization not achieved or runtime regresses by >5% vs baseline
Numeric checks fail under selected compiler flags/pragmas
